title: Contact | Sarah Owen MP
h1: Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH
headings: h2: Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Sarah Owen MP at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN
text_excerpt: --> Contact | Sarah Owen MP top of page Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH HOME ABOUT CAMPAIGNS CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. CONTACT ME If you live in Luton North, I am here to help. Queries that I take up on behalf of local residents are called "casework". Ways I can help include: Housing, homelessness and tenancy support Benefits and other welfare payments Asylum, immigration and citizenship Local campaigns and support groups Education Health ​ When emailing, please include your full name and postal address . Parliamentary protocol dictates that I can only assist residents who live in Luton North. Click here to check if I am your MP. ​ My office treats everyone with respect and courtesy, and expect the same in return. You can read my office standards here . envelope sarah.owen.mp@parliament.uk We aim to respond to urgent casework as soon as possible. Please note there may be a delay in response to campaign or policy emails. phone 01582 488208 The office phone line is open from Monday to Friday, 10am to 5pm, excluding bank holidays. envelope-open-text 256 Marsh Road, Luton, LU3 2RX Please note that the office is not open to the public and visits are strictly by appointment only. ​ ​​ I know that many constituents may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to generate emails before sending them on to me. I know it can be a helpful tool. However there are limitations to be aware of that may make it more time efficient to email me directly, if you can. These limitations include: AI emails are generally longer, and thus take more time to process and respond to AI can be inaccurate in the steps it thinks an MP has taken or can take to help with or progress a case .AI may suggest an MP’s office take steps that would be unhelpful or counter-productive. AI may not fully be able to understand a response my office has sent and thus more correspondence (and more processing time) is required to respond to and progress cases. Use of AI What happens when I contact you for help? If you email my main Parliamentary email account, a member of my team will log your case with our caseworker software and make any inquiries on your behalf to assist with the matter as best we can. If you telephone our office, we will ask that you either email or fill out a written consent form for us to make representations on your behalf. We aim to reply to all emails and phone calls promptly. Please email media requests to my main Parliamentary inbox. For urgent queries please phone the office. Media enquiries WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH bluesky Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Sarah Owen MP at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN © 2025 Office of Sarah Owen MP bottom of page
